title: neonauclea lanceolata subsp. gracilis
intro: Neonauclea lanceolata subsp. gracilis, also known as the gracilis species of the Neonauclea lanceolata plant, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family that is native to the tropical forests of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antibacterial properties, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Neonauclea lanceolata subsp. gracilis include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of malaria, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or an infusion, which can be taken as a herbal tea or used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
